title: Find perimeter when given side lengths
description: Find perimeter of figures when given an image or context.
topic_path: Get ready for area and perimeter
Find perimeter when given side lengths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the triangle?**  



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding the lengths of all three sides:


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleC{21} + \maroonD{27}+ \greenD{14}$   
or  
$\maroonD{27}+ \greenD{14}+  \purpleC{21} $

options.alt: A triangle with each side labeled as follows: 14 meters, 27 meters, and 21 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the shape?**  
  *All sides are the same length.*



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In the given shape, all sides are the same length.    

Therefore, all $\greenD {\text{six sides}}$ measure $\blueD {{4} \text{ cm}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter by either adding the lengths of all $\greenD {\text{six sides}}$:    

$\blueD {4} + \blueD{4 }+ \blueD {4} + \blueD {4} + \blueD {4} + \blueD {4}$  

Or by multiplying  $\greenD {6}$ by $\blueD4$:  

$\greenD6 \times \blueD4$

options.alt: A six-sided shape with all sides the same length. One side is labeled 4 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the shape?**    
*All sides are the same length.*



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In the given shape, all sides are the same length.    
Therefore, all $\greenD {\text{five sides}}$ measure $\blueD {{7} \text{ cm}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter by either adding the lengths of all $\greenD {\text{five sides}}$:    

$\blueD {7} + \blueD {7} + \blueD {7} + \blueD {7} + \blueD {7}$  

Or by multiplying  $\greenD5$ by $\blueD7$:  

$\greenD5 {} \times \blueD7$

options.alt: A five-sided shape with each side of equal length. One side is labeled 7 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the shape?**    
*All sides are the same length.*


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In the given shape, all sides are the same length.    
Therefore, all $\greenD{\text{six sides}}$ measure $\blueD{15\text{ cm}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter by either adding the lengths of all $\greenD {\text{six sides}}$:    

$\blueD {15} + \blueD{15}+ \blueD {15} + \blueD {15} + \blueD {15} + \blueD {15}$  

Or by multiplying  $\greenD {6}$ by $\blueD{15}$:  

$\greenD6 \times \blueD {15}$

options.alt: A six-sided shape with sides of equal length. One side is labeled 15 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the square?**    
*All sides are the same length.*  



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In a square, all sides are the same length.    
Therefore, all $\greenD {\text{four sides}}$ measure $\blueD {{9} \text{ cm}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter either by adding the lengths of all $\greenD {\text{four sides}}$:    

$\blueD {9} + \blueD{9}+ \blueD {9} + \blueD {9}$  

Or by multiplying  $\greenD4{}$ by $\blueD9$:  

$\greenD4 \times \blueD {9} $

options.alt: A square. One side is labeled 9 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the shape?**  



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In the given shape, opposite sides have equal lengths.    

Therefore, both top and bottom measure $\purpleD {{14} \text{ m}}$, and both sides measure $\greenD {{20}\text{ m}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter either by adding the lengths of all four sides:    

$\purpleC {14} + \purpleC {14} + \greenD {20} + \greenD {20}$  

Or by multiplying $2$ by $\purpleC {14}$ and $2$ by $\greenD {20}$ and then adding the two products:  

$(2 \times \purpleC {14})  + (2 \times \greenD {20})$

options.alt: A four-sided shape with sides labeled as follows: 14 meters, 20 meters, 14 meters, and 20 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions can be used to find the perimeter of the rectangle?**  


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

In a rectangle, opposite sides have equal lengths.   
 
Therefore, both top and bottom measure $\purpleC {{11} \text{ m}}$, and both sides measure $\greenD {{17}\text{ m}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the perimeter either by adding the lengths of all four sides:    

$\purpleC {11} + \purpleC {11} + \greenD {17} + \greenD {17}$  

Or by multiplying $2$ by $\purpleC {11}+\greenD {17}$:  

$2 \times (\purpleC {11}+\greenD {17}) $

options.alt: A rectangle with one width labeled 17 meters and one length labeled 11 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the triangle?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD8 + \greenD6+\purpleC{10}$
The perimeter is $24$ centimeters.

options.alt: A triangle with side lengths labeled as follows: 6 centimeters, 8 centimeters and 10 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The length of each side of the star is $8$ units.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the star?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{8} + \greenD{8}+\purpleC{8}+\maroonD{8}+\tealD{8}+\goldE{8}+\redD{8}+\blueB{8}+\greenB{8}+\purpleA{8}$

Since the star has $10$ sides that all are $\blueD{8\,\text{units}}$ long, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = 10 \times \blueD{8}$
The perimeter is $80$ units.

options.alt: A star created with 10 sides of equal length.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the quadrilateral?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{11} + \greenD{9}+\purpleC{5}+\maroonD{7}$
The perimeter is $32$ meters.

options.alt: A four-sided figure with each side labeled as follows: 11 meters, 9 meters, 5 meters, and 7 meters.

**What is the perimeter of the triangle?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{55} + \greenD{36}+\purpleC{48}$
The perimeter is $139$ centimeters.

options.alt: A triangle with three sides labeled as 55 centimeters, 36 centimeters, and 48 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the figure?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{5} + \greenD{4}+\purpleC{4}+\maroonD{5}+\tealD{4}$
The perimeter is $22$ centimeters.

options.alt: A five-sided figure with each side labeled as follows: 5 centimeters, 4 centimeters, 4 centimeters, 5 centimeters, and 4 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the trapezoid?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{18} + \greenD{8}+\purpleC{10}+\maroonD{10}$

options.alt: A four-sided shape with sides labeled as follows: 18 meters, 8 meters, 10 meters, and 10 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
One side of a rectangle is $8$ units.  Another side is $5$ units. 

**What is the perimeter of the rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD8 +\purpleC5+ \greenD8+\purpleC5$
The perimeter of the rectangle is $26$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
One side of a regular pentagon is $7$ units.

**What is the perimeter of the pentagon?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  

All the sides of a *regular* pentagon are the same length.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD{7}+\greenD{7}+\greenD{7}+\greenD{7}+\greenD{7}$

There are $\blueD{5\,\text{sides}}$ that are each $\greenD{7\,\text{units}}$ long.  So, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{5} \times \greenD{7}$
The perimeter of the regular pentagon is $35$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
One side of a regular triangle is $5$ units.

**What is the perimeter of the triangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  

All the sides of a *regular* triangle are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD{5}+\greenD{5}+\greenD{5}$

There are $\blueD{3\,\text{sides}}$ that are each $\greenD{5\,\text{units}}$ long.  So, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{3} \times \greenD{5}$
The perimeter of the regular triangle is $15$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
One side of a regular hexagon is $9$ units.

**What is the perimeter of the hexagon?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  


All the sides of a *regular* hexagon are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD{9}+\greenD{9}+\greenD{9}+\greenD{9}+\greenD{9}+\greenD{9}$

There are $\blueD{6\,\text{sides}}$ that are each $\greenD{9\,\text{units}}$ long.  So, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{6} \times \greenD{9}$
The perimeter of the regular hexagon is $54$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the regular pentagon?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  

All sides of a *regular* pentagon are the same length.




[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD9+\greenD9+\greenD9+\greenD9+\greenD9$

Since there are $\blueD5$ sides that are all $\greenD9$ centimeters long, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD5\times\greenD9$
The perimeter of the pentagon is $45$ centimeters.

options.alt: A five-sided shape with all sides the same length. One side is labeled 9 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the regular hexagon?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  

All sides of a *regular* hexagon are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \greenD{3}+\greenD{3}+\greenD{3}+\greenD{3}+\greenD{3}+\greenD{3}$

Since there are $\blueD{6}$ sides that are all $\greenD{3}$ centimeters long, we can also multiply.

$\text{Perimeter} = \blueD{6}\times\greenD{3}$
The perimeter of the hexagon is $18$ meters.

options.alt: A six-sided figure with all sides the same length. One side is labeled 3 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the perimeter of the figure?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter} = \purpleC{9}+ \greenD{15}+\purpleC{9}+\greenD{15} $
The perimeter of the figure is $48$ centimeters.

options.alt: A four-sided figure with a length side labeled as 15 centimeters and a width side labeled as 9 centimeters.

[[☃ image 1]]

hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

We can find the perimeter by adding all of the side lengths.  


[[☃ image 1]]
